title: Ensure customer-managed keys (CMK) are used to encrypt  data at rest on Azure Elastic SAN volume groups

input_row.title: Ensure customer-managed keys (CMK) are used to encrypt  data at rest on Azure Elastic SAN volume groups
input_row.description: Azure Elastic SAN volume groups offer two encryption options: Microsoft-managed keys, which provide automatic encryption without user intervention, and customer- managed keys (CMK), which allow organizations to retain full control over their encryption keys for enhanced security and compliance.
input_row.rationale: Using customer-managed keys (CMKs) to encrypt Azure Elastic SAN volume groups enhances security by granting organizations complete control over their encryption keys.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Elastic SANs. 2. Click the name of an Elastic SAN. 3. Under SAN Management, click Volume groups. 4. For each volume group, ensure that the value in the Encryption type column is not Platform-managed key. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list Elastic SANs: az elastic-san list For each Elastic SAN with a volumeGroupCount greater than 0, run the following command to list volume groups: az elastic-san volume-group list --resource-group --elastic- san Ensure that for each volume group, encryption is set to EncryptionAtRestWithCustomerManagedKey. Audit from PowerShell Run the following command to list Elastic SANs: Get-AzElasticSan For each Elastic SAN with a VolumeGroupCount greater than 0, run the following command to list volume groups: Get-AzElasticSanVolumeGroup -ResourceGroupName - ElasticSanName Ensure that for each volume group, Encryption is set to EncryptionAtRestWithCustomerManagedKey. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 7698f4ed-80ce-4e13-b408-ee135fa400a5 • Name: 'ElasticSan Volume Group should use customer-managed keys to encrypt data at rest'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal It is not currently possible to remediate from Azure Portal. Refer to the Remediate from Azure CLI and Remediate from PowerShell sections for remediation options. Remediate from Azure CLI For each volume group requiring remediation, run the following command to assign an identity: az elastic-san volume-group update --resource-group -- elastic-san --volume-group --identity "{type:SystemAssigned}" Note: Use --identity '{type:UserAssigned,user-assigned-identity:""}' with the above command to provide a UserAssigned Identity Id. For each volume group requiring remediation, run the following command to assign a customer-managed encryption key: az elastic-san volume-group update --resource-group -- elastic-san --volume-group --encryption EncryptionAtRestWithCustomerManagedKey --encryption-properties "{key-vault- properties:{key-name:'',key-vault-uri:''}}" Remediate from PowerShell For each volume group requiring remediation, run the following command to assign an identity: Update-AzElasticSanVolumeGroup -ResourceGroupName - ElasticSanName -VolumeGroupName -IdentityType SystemAssigned Note: Use -IdentityType UserAssigned -IdentityUserAssignedIdentityId with the above command to provide a UserAssigned Identity Id. For each volume group requiring remediation, run the following command to assign a customer-managed encryption key: Update-AzElasticSanVolumeGroup -ResourceGroupName - ElasticSanName -VolumeGroupName -Encryption EncryptionAtRestWithCustomerManagedKey -KeyName -KeyVaultUri
input_row.impact: There are costs and configuration overhead associated with setting up and managing customer-managed keys.
input_row.default_value: By default, Azure Elastic SAN volume groups are encrypted using Microsoft-managed keys.
input_row.additional_information: • To enable encryption, it is necessary to grant the volume group the appropriate permissions to access the encryption key in the key vault. The key can be modified as needed. Refer to the following guide for details: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/elastic-san/elastic-san-configure- customer-managed-keys?#configure-the-key-vault. • Azure Elastic SAN uses system-assigned managed identities and user-assigned managed identities to authenticate the volume groups to access encryption keys stored in Azure Key Vault. Refer to the following guide for details: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/elastic-san/elastic-san-configure- customer-managed-keys#choose-a-managed-identity-to-authorize-access-to-the- key-vault.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_elastic_beanstalk_environment_encryption_at_rest_with_customer_managed_keys

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
1. **API Calls:** 
   - Use `boto3` to access AWS Elastic Beanstalk environments and check encryption settings:
     - Implement `boto3.client('elasticbeanstalk').describe_environments()` to fetch environment details.
     - Fetch S3 bucket settings if storage resources are involved.
   
2. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - `aws elasticbeanstalk describe-environments` to list environments.
   - Additional commands to check if S3 buckets are being used for storage and verify their encryption settings:
     - `aws s3api get-bucket-encryption --bucket <bucket_name>` to ensure the bucket is using customer-managed keys.

3. **Resources to Enumerate:**
   - Identify all Elastic Beanstalk environments within the specified accounts and regions.

4. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Ensure that all Elastic Beanstalk environments are using encryption that is configured to leverage customer-managed keys rather than default AWS-managed keys.

5. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - The compliance check passes if the property containing `EncryptionAtRestWithCustomerManagedKey` is confirmed; otherwise, it fails.

6. **Example:**
   - Implement a check that lists all Elastic Beanstalk environments and verifies each environment’s associated storage settings to confirm proper encryption configurations are in place.
